general_notes: Configuration for AI/ML conference validation table. Focus on accuracy for event details, pricing, and relevance scoring. All dates should be in YYYY-MM-DD format. Pricing should include currency (USD) and specify if TBA/sold out status. For venue information, search for specific facility names and addresses. Format should be clearly specified (In-person, Hybrid, Virtual, etc.).

search_groups.description: Core conference identification fields used for context
Date, location, venue, and format information typically found together in event announcements
Pricing, size, audience, and themes information usually found in conference marketing materials
Website, sponsor info, and submission details typically found on conference websites
Relevance scoring and internal notes requiring business context evaluation

validation_targets.description: Short conference name or acronym with year
validation_targets.notes: Primary identifier for the conference event

validation_targets.description: Full official name of the conference series
examples: ACM SIGIR on Research and Development in Information Retrieval
validation_targets.notes: Complete formal name of the conference series

validation_targets.description: Year when the conference takes place

validation_targets.notes: Format as YYYY-MM-DD. Search official conference websites and announcements for confirmed dates.
Format as YYYY-MM-DD. May be same as start date for single-day events. Verify against official sources.

validation_targets.description: City where the conference is held
validation_targets.notes: Include state/province if needed for clarity (e.g., Boise, ID)

validation_targets.description: Country or region where the conference takes place
validation_targets.notes: Use country names or recognized regional designations

validation_targets.description: Specific venue or facility name where conference is held
validation_targets.notes: Official venue name as listed by conference organizers. Search for specific facility names and addresses. Use 'TBA' only if venue is genuinely not yet announced.

validation_targets.description: Conference delivery format (in-person, hybrid, virtual, etc.)
validation_targets.notes: Include special access requirements if applicable (e.g., invite-only). Use specific format terms: 'In-person', 'Hybrid', 'Virtual', etc. Avoid 'TBA' unless format is genuinely undecided.
Include USD currency, specify if TBA (To Be Announced) or sold out status

validation_targets.description: Estimated or announced conference attendance size
validation_targets.notes: Include source context (announced, past year data, targeted, etc.)

examples: AI engineers, founders, VPs of AI
AI engineers, infra teams, enterprise buyers, investors
validation_targets.notes: Focus on professional roles and industry segments

validation_targets.description: Main technical themes and topics covered at the conference
examples: Agentic AI, LLM engineering, evals, toolchains
Applied ML, LLMops, recsys, causal inference
validation_targets.notes: List key technical areas and trending topics

validation_targets.description: Sponsorship and exhibition information or URLs
validation_targets.notes: Use 'N/A' if no sponsor/expo information available, otherwise provide URL or description

validation_targets.description: Call for Papers and submission deadline information
examples: Abstract deadline Sept 19, 2025; full paper Sept 26, 2025
validation_targets.notes: Include key submission deadlines and process notes

validation_targets.description: Internal relevance score from 1 (low) to 5 (high) for Hyperplexity business interests
validation_targets.notes: Integer scale 1-5, where 5 indicates highest strategic relevance. Consider conference themes, audience alignment with AI/search technology, and business development opportunities.

validation_targets.description: Additional internal notes about conference relevance and strategic value
examples: Broad AI; high signal for agentic AI and evaluation.
Crossroads of IR, LLMs, knowledge & web agents.
validation_targets.notes: Internal strategic assessment and business development notes

storage_metadata.description: Configuration for AI/ML conference validation table. Focus on accuracy for event details, pricing, and relevance scoring. All dates should be in YYYY-MM-DD format. Pricing should include currency (USD) and specify if TBA/sold out status. For venue information, search for specific facility names and addresses. Format should be clearly specified (In-person, Hybrid, Virtual, etc.).
